message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/click/shell_completion.py,src/click/core.py
summary: Fixing resource leak in shell completion by wrapping make_context calls in _resolve_context with 'with' statements

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
summary: Adding validate_nesting param to Context for nested context validation (resource leak prevention)

type: STATUS
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
summary: Done: added validate_nesting param+_children tracking to Context (core.py __init__/close/_make_sub_context), and set validate_nesting=False in shell_completion.py _resolve_context's 3 make_context calls. Did not touch actual make_context call sites' with-statement wrapping, so your leak fix there shouldn't conflict but check for merge overlap on those same call sites in _resolve_context.

type: STATUS
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
summary: Done: wrapped all 3 make_context calls in _resolve_context (top-level cli.make_context, non-chain cmd.make_context, chained cmd.make_context) with 'with...as ctx' statements so Context.__exit__/close() runs, fixing file handle ResourceWarning leaks. Also updated Context.exit() docstring in core.py. If your validate_nesting sets that flag as a kwarg on the same make_context calls, they should merge fine since I only added 'with' wrapping around the existing calls, not changed their kwargs.
